findings.evidence: Lines 66-68 define the map with argument `a` and body `Finsupp.linearCombination 𝕜 (normalizedChebyshevTLp 𝕜) a`; lines 78-93 then expose manual zero/add/smul lemmas that would be inherited from a bundled `LinearMap`.
findings.fix: Define `chebyshevModeLinearCombination : (ℕ →₀ 𝕜) →ₗ[𝕜] Lp 𝕜 2 Polynomial.Chebyshev.measureT := Finsupp.linearCombination 𝕜 (normalizedChebyshevTLp 𝕜)`, keep an apply/sum characterization, and restate the coordinate and injectivity lemmas against the bundled map.
findings.issue: `chebyshevModeLinearCombination` is exposed as a function `(ℕ →₀ 𝕜) → Lp ...`, while the implementation is `Finsupp.linearCombination`, a bundled linear map; this makes the public API weaker than the object it denotes and forces ad hoc add/smul lemmas instead of the standard `LinearMap` interface.

summary: The finite Chebyshev expansion API exposes the expansion only as an unbundled function, even though its own body and lemmas are the bundled linear-combination map. Downstream finite-coordinate users lose the standard `LinearMap` interface for kernels, ranges, composition, and map lemmas.
